data.title: Iran Threatens to Target Steel Plants in Gulf Region and Israel
data.description: Iran has issued a warning about potential attacks on steel plants located in the Gulf region and Israel. According to Odaily, this announcement was reported by Iran's Tasnim News Agency. The warning highlights escalating tensions in the region, raising concerns over the security of industrial facilities.

data.title: Nouriel Roubini Warns of Potential U.S.-Iran Conflict Impact on Federal Reserve Rates
data.description: Economist Nouriel Roubini has expressed concerns about the possibility of U.S. President Donald Trump expanding military actions against Iran. According to NS3.AI, Roubini estimates there is a greater than 50% chance of such an escalation occurring. He cautioned that this could lead to a situation reminiscent of the stagflation experienced in the 1970s, potentially prompting the Federal Reserve to consider a rate hike.

data.title: Walrus Achieves 450TB Data Milestone in First Year
data.description: Walrus has announced that it has exceeded 450 terabytes of stored data within its first year following the mainnet launch. According to NS3.AI, this achievement was supported by partnerships with organizations such as Team Liquid, Decrypt, and Allium, which contributed to the platform's growth.

data.title: Iran says it is prepared to respond to any form of ground invasion.
data.description: In response to the recent troop buildup by the US military in the Middle East, Iran has stated that it is prepared to respond to any form of ground invasion. According to Iranian media reports on March 26, Iran has mobilized over one million combat personnel in preparation for a ground war with the United States. (CCTV International News)

data.title: ECB Paper Highlights Concentration in DeFi Governance
data.description:
A recent paper by the European Central Bank has revealed significant concentration in the governance of decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols. According to NS3.AI, the study found that the top 100 addresses hold over 80% of the voting power in major protocols like Aave, MakerDAO, and Uniswap. Many of these addresses are reportedly controlled by protocols or exchanges.

The paper also highlighted that delegates play a dominant role in voting processes, with approximately one-third of key voters remaining unidentified. This situation poses regulatory challenges and raises questions about the decentralization of decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs). The findings suggest a need for increased transparency and potential regulatory oversight in the DeFi space.

data.title: Ether Faces Potential Deeper Correction Amid Structural Weakness
data.description:
Ether's recent decline below the $2,000 mark on Friday has raised concerns about a possible deeper correction in the coming weeks or months. According to Cointelegraph, the cryptocurrency's price is showing signs of structural weakness as it struggles to maintain the psychological support level of $2,000. Analysts suggest that Ether's price may continue to fall towards the $1,750-$1,850 support zone, with negative demand further increasing its downward potential.

Data from TradingView indicates that ETH/USD was trading at $1,975, marking a 5% decrease over the last 24 hours. This drop coincided with over $111 million in long Ether liquidations. Earlier in the week, the pair failed to break through the $2,200 resistance, as spot Ether exchange-traded fund (ETF) outflows, declining decentralized exchange (DEX) volumes, and a reduced ETH futures premium hindered its recovery. Trader Onur noted in a Friday X post that despite strong long-term narratives, short-term demand remains thin. Fellow analyst CryptoWZRD predicted a further decline towards the $1,800 support zone after Ether closed below $2,200 on Thursday. Analyst and trader Ted Pillows echoed this sentiment, stating that the drop below $2,100 signals weakness and foreshadows further declines for ETH.

As reported by Cointelegraph, a close below the 50-day simple moving average at $2,000 could lead the ETH/USD pair to $1,900 and subsequently to the $1,850-$1,750 level. Ether's apparent demand has reached a 16-month low, flipping negative due to traders adopting a risk-off stance amid geopolitical uncertainty and macroeconomic challenges. Capriole Investment's Ethereum Apparent Demand metric reveals that demand for ETH has been negative since March 3, bottoming at -58,000 ETH on March 16, marking a 16-month low. The metric has since improved to -23,475 ETH at the time of writing.

Meanwhile, spot ETH ETFs have recorded net outflows for seven consecutive days, totaling $391.8 million. Global Ether exchange-traded products (ETPs) also experienced $27.2 million in outflows last week, highlighting a reduced appetite for ETH among institutional investors. These developments underscore the challenges facing Ether as it navigates a period of structural weakness and declining demand.

data.title: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest heavily sold off tech stocks and Bitcoin ETFs.
data.description: Ark Invest sold approximately $41 million worth of Meta stock and over $26 million worth of Nvidia stock on Thursday. In addition, the company sold approximately $11 million of its own Bitcoin ETF (ARKB) shares, approximately $6.5 million worth of stock in the crypto exchange Bullish, and nearly $5 million worth of stock in Block, a company owned by Jack Dorsey. Ark Invest also reduced its holdings in Alphabet stock by approximately $2.5 million and AMD stock by approximately $7.5 million. Meta's stock price has fallen more than 17% over the past month to approximately $531, including a drop of about 10% in the past week, after the platform lost two lawsuits related to social media addiction. Nvidia's stock price has fallen about 5% over the past month while facing a class-action lawsuit regarding discrepancies in crypto mining revenue. Bitcoin fell about 4.8% in the past 24 hours to approximately $66,020, briefly dipping below $66,000, its lowest level since March 2nd. Following the sell-off, Ark Invest holds approximately $100 million in ARKB, making it the 35th largest holding among its actively managed ETFs. Cathie Wood had previously predicted that Bitcoin could reach $1.2 million per coin by 2030.

data.title: A16z Crypto Partner: The crypto industry is entering the "shirt era," and the integration of blockchain and AI will drive on-chain agent payments.
data.description: Guy Wuollet, a partner at a16z crypto, stated on Fortune's Crypto Playbook program that the crypto industry is maturing and currently in its "shirt age"—not yet fully formalized, but already past its early stages. He pointed out that the dominant trend in the current crypto industry is Wall Street's rapid adoption of blockchain, with traditional financial giants like BlackRock and Fidelity not only offering Bitcoin ETFs but also embracing blockchain technology in stablecoins and tokenized stocks. Wuollet also noted that the integration of blockchain and AI is laying the foundation for on-chain proxy commerce. He stated that a large number of talents from the consumer AI field are entering the crypto industry because they believe the first proxy payments will occur on-chain, and Stripe also discussed proxy payments in its annual letter. Regarding the potential fragmentation caused by different payment standards, Wuollet believes that AI models excel at understanding different systems, so this is unlikely to slow down adoption.

data.title: Ripple CEO Warns Against Weaponizing Crypto Policy
data.description:
Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse has cautioned against the weaponization of cryptocurrency policy during an interview on 'Mornings with Maria.' According to Foresight News, Garlinghouse emphasized the importance of avoiding another moment similar to that of Gary Gensler.

Garlinghouse also addressed Ripple's growth amid market volatility, the new framework from the SEC and CFTC, and the CLARITY Act.

data.title: Iran's Heavy Water Reactor Targeted in Airstrike
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Iranian media Fars News reported on March 27 that Iran's Khondab heavy water research reactor was targeted in an airstrike. The attack did not result in any casualties, and nearby residents were not at risk.

data.title: Ethereum Tests Key Fibonacci Level Amid Market Activity
data.description: Ethereum is currently trading at $2,068, testing the $2,055 Fibonacci level. According to NS3.AI, mid-tier holders have sold approximately 370,000 ETH, while larger whale cohorts have absorbed this supply. The $2,000 mark is considered a crucial threshold for Ethereum's next potential move.

data.title: Spot gold broke through $4,530/ounce, while spot silver surged $3 intraday.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold broke through $4,530 per ounce, up 3.47% on the day. Spot silver surged $3.00 on the day, currently trading at $71.12 per ounce, up 4.41%.

data.title: Iran's heavy water research reactor was attacked by air.
data.description: According to Iranian media outlet Fars News, Iranian officials stated that the Khondab heavy water research reactor was attacked by air. Officials said the attack caused no casualties and nearby residents are not facing any security threats. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran's Response to U.S. Peace Proposal Expected Today
data.description: According to BlockBeats, multiple sources have indicated that Iran is expected to announce its response to a U.S. peace proposal later today, as reported by CBS News on March 27. The proposal aims to address ongoing tensions between the two nations. Details of the response and its implications remain to be seen.

data.title: White House Launches Official App to Enhance Information Transparency
data.description: The White House has officially launched its app, 'The White House App,' aimed at providing live streams and real-time information. According to Foresight News, this initiative seeks to offer the public direct access to unfiltered information, enhancing transparency and directness in government communication.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Barkin Considers March Rate Hold as Prudent
data.description: Federal Reserve official Barkin has expressed that maintaining interest rates unchanged in March appears to be a prudent decision. According to ChainCatcher, Barkin's comments reflect a cautious approach amid ongoing economic evaluations. The decision aligns with the Federal Reserve's strategy to assess economic conditions before making further adjustments to monetary policy.

data.title: Federal Reserve to Monitor Inflation for Future Policy Decisions
data.description: Federal Reserve official Barkin has stated that the institution will closely observe changes in inflation and expectations data to guide future monetary policy decisions. According to ChainCatcher, this approach aims to ensure that the Federal Reserve remains responsive to economic conditions as they evolve.

data.title: Lido Proposal: To Use Up to 10,000 stETH to Buy Back LDO
data.description: According to the Lido community forum, the Lido Growth Committee has proposed authorizing the use of up to 10,000 stETH from the Lido DAO treasury to purchase LDO in batches through on-exchange and off-exchange channels. Currently, the LDO/ETH ratio is approximately 0.00016, a discount of about 63% from the two-year median of 0.00043, while the protocol's net return has only decreased by about 20% during the same period. Regarding the execution mechanism, each batch is capped at 1,000 stETH, with a price cap and a maximum slippage tolerance threshold of 3%. A report must be published on the forum after each batch is completed before the next batch can be initiated. Trading channels include on-chain CoW Swap, 1inch, Uniswap, and centralized exchanges such as Binance, OKX, and Bybit, and market makers are allowed to be delegated for execution. All purchased LDO will be returned to the DAO treasury, and holders cannot participate in any governance voting during the holding period. The proposal identifies key risks including front-running attacks, smart contract vulnerabilities, market volatility, and CEX fund freezes, and mitigates these risks through mechanisms such as decentralized execution channels, setting strict thresholds, and retaining the DAO's ability to terminate authorization at any time.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Confidence Index Falls to Three-Month Low
data.description:
The University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index for March has dropped to a final reading of 53.3, marking a three-month low. According to RTHK, this figure is below the preliminary reading of 55.5 and February's final value of 56.6, with the decline exceeding expectations.

The survey indicates that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East has driven up oil prices and caused significant volatility in financial markets, heightening consumer concerns about the economic outlook. This has led to a weakening in consumer confidence, particularly among middle- to high-income individuals and those with stock assets.

Inflation expectations for the coming year have risen to a final value of 3.8%, surpassing both the preliminary and February final readings of 3.4%. However, the five-year inflation expectation has slightly decreased to 3.2%.

data.title: HSBC Lowers Tesla Price Target, Predicts Significant Downside
data.description: HSBC has revised its 12-month price target for Tesla, reducing it from $133 to $119 while maintaining a Sell rating. According to NS3.AI, this adjustment suggests a potential downside of approximately 70% from Tesla's current share price of around $381.
